title: Dead Code, Unused Dependencies, and Reachability · Policy Workshop
summary: Remove unreachable code and dependencies with evidence rather than guesswork. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a dead-code and dependency audit while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Treat unused imports, variables, parameters, private members, and exports according to language semantics.
Detect unreachable branches, impossible states, and obsolete feature paths.
Detect unused, missing, duplicate, and undeclared dependencies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a gates-safe-removal-1256aw: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Safe Removal into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-safe-removal-1256aw:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
